stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced April 20, 2026: net profit +34.89% YoY to Rs 2,014 cr; NII +18.81% to Rs 3,702 cr. FY26 PAT Rs 7,019 cr (+27.17%); operating profit Rs 10,826 cr (+16.41%). Total business +17.47% YoY to Rs 6.42 lakh cr; deposits +14.14% to Rs 3.51 lakh cr; gross advances +21.74% to Rs 2.92 lakh cr. GNPA improved to 1.45% (vs 1.74% YoY); NNPA 0.13%. SMA1+2 down 61 bps to 1.39%; total stress 2.93%. Final dividend Rs 1.20/share (in addition to Rs 1.00 interim already paid). Created Rs 200 cr provision for global geopolitical uncertainties. Stock around Rs 79.27 on April 27.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty PSU Bank Index trading near 9,032 (Apr 21) vs 52w high 9,919 and low 5,904. PSU banks consistently outperforming private peers in recent months on improving credit growth and asset quality, supported by RBI rate-cut cycle (repo at 5.25%). Banking indices gained over 1% on Apr 21 with broad participation across both PSU and private lenders.
stocks.peer_comparison: Among the strongest PSU bank prints this season: 21.74% advances growth and 0.13% NNPA are best-in-class within the PSU pack. Profit growth of ~35% YoY ahead of larger peers (SBI weighting 51.8% in PSU index). Loan-deposit growth gap (22% vs 14%) wider than most peers - watch funding pressure.
red_flags: MarketsMojo flagged 'asset quality concerns' despite headline GNPA improvement
Management cited concerns over crude >$100/bbl risk to MSME book
Loan growth (+21.7%) materially outpacing deposit growth (+14.1%) - CD ratio pressure
Stock has rallied substantially - Q4 catalyst likely already priced in
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 1.20/share record date pending
FY27 credit growth and NIM guidance from earnings call follow-up
Continued PSU bank sector momentum with further RBI cuts possible

stocks.recent_news: Q4/FY26 results announced April 20, 2026: FY26 PAT record Rs 2,291 cr (+18.3% YoY); EPS Rs 87.94 (+20.3%); NII +13.1% to Rs 3,110 cr. Loan assets Rs 87,347 cr (+15.3% YoY); FY26 disbursements Rs 26,548 cr (+20.8%). Retail loan book Rs 86,946 cr (+16% YoY). GNPA dropped to 0.93% - first time below 1% (signals improved underwriting and recoveries). Roshni (affordable housing) segment driving higher yields with disciplined underwriting. Final dividend Rs 8/share announced. Stock surged 8.92% to Rs 987.65 in single session on Apr 21. Earnings call transcript released Apr 25.
stocks.sector_context: India home loan market USD 430.74 bn (2026), projected USD 809.07 bn by 2031 at 13.44% CAGR. NBFCs growing fastest at 18.38% CAGR; affordable housing (<Rs 25 lakh) sub-segment growing 22-23%. Repo at 5.25% (Dec 2025) restored affordability. Home-loan AUM expected to grow 12-13%, LAP 20%+. Public-sector banks pass on rate cuts faster via external benchmark linkage; HFCs compete via digital onboarding and underwriting speed.
stocks.peer_comparison: Loan growth of 15.3% above industry; GNPA at 0.93% best-in-class among listed HFCs; disbursement growth +20.8% suggests share gains. Roshni affordable-housing pivot aligns with high-growth peers. PNB (promoter) holds 32.57% stake.
red_flags: ASM/GSM history flagged as recently as Jan 23, 2026 - watch current surveillance status
PNB promoter at 32.57% creates supply overhang risk if any future stake reduction
Stock rallied 8.92% on results day - profit-booking risk near term
Faster bank rate-cut transmission could pressure HFC NIMs
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 8/share record date pending
Re-rating potential with sustained sub-1% GNPA
Continued mix-shift to affordable housing for higher yields
Branch expansion and digital underwriting momentum

stocks.recent_news: ETF priced around Rs 123.94 on April 24, 2026. AUM Rs 54,128 cr (largest Indian gold ETF); expense ratio 0.80%. MCX gold at Rs 1,52,799 per 10 g on Apr 27 (+0.7% / +Rs 1,038 day). Spot gold around Rs 15,404/g (24K), Rs 14,120/g (22K), Rs 11,553/g (18K). Domestic gold partially recovered after a sharp Rs 22,300/10g correction in prior weeks. Prices hovering near record highs amid USD weakness, central-bank buying, geopolitical risk and steady wedding-season demand.
stocks.sector_context: Gold in volatile near-term regime - mainstream 2026 forecasts target Rs 1.28-1.38 lakh per 10g; bullish case Rs 1.7-2.0 lakh per 10g if Fed cuts more aggressively or geopolitical risk premium expands. Supportive: weaker USD, central-bank accumulation (RBI included), inflation hedging, steady physical demand into wedding season. Counter: prices have already run hard; correction risk if risk-on rotation accelerates.
stocks.peer_comparison: Largest and most liquid gold ETF in India with tight tracking error vs domestic gold. Mid-pack expense ratio (0.80%) vs HDFC, ICICI Prudential, Kotak gold ETFs. Pure-beta gold exposure - no idiosyncratic alpha vs peers.
red_flags: Recent Rs 22,300/10g drawdown indicates elevated volatility
Prices near record highs - mean-reversion risk from any USD strength or risk-on shift
Analyst commentary suggests 'fluctuations rather than clear directional trend' near term
Akshaya Tritiya season largely past - immediate physical demand support fades
catalysts: Geopolitical risk premium (e.g., Iran tensions) persistence

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced April 21, 2026: net profit Rs 1,110.9 cr (+27.18% YoY); revenue Rs 6,747.79 cr (+22.6% YoY) - strongest quarterly revenue in a decade. EBITDA margin 26.3%. Beat consensus PAT estimate (Rs 990-1,010 cr) and revenue (Rs 6,230-6,370 cr) materially. Volume-led growth ~18%, broad-based across MAGGI, KITKAT, NESCAFE; gains widespread across categories and channels despite >50% A&P spend hike. FY26 sales Rs 230.72 bn (+14.90%); cash from ops Rs 50.48 bn; full-year EBITDA margin 23.0%. Final dividend Rs 5/share, record date July 10, 2026. Stock hit 52w high Rs 1,374.70 on Apr 21 (+6.86%).
stocks.sector_context: Nifty FMCG rallied 2.55% on results day, led by Nestle, HUL, Varun Beverages. CRISIL projects FMCG revenue growth 6-8% in FY26 (vs 5-6% in FY25). Rural demand outpacing urban (Q1 2025 rural growth 4x urban). Tailwinds: easing inflation, GST reforms, benign commodities, monetary easing, tax relief. Risk: global geopolitical tensions (Iran).
stocks.peer_comparison: Materially outperformed FMCG peers in Q4 - 22.6% revenue and ~18% volume growth vs sector mid-single-digit. P/E 73.8x at premium to HUL 54.4x and Britannia 56.5x. ROE 66.55% trails only Britannia (74.67%); peer-group average ~29%. Market cap Rs 2.73 trn (Apr 22) vs HUL Rs 5.41 trn and Britannia Rs 1.37 trn.
red_flags: Premium P/E 73.8x leaves limited cushion if growth normalises
>50% A&P spend hike sustainability questionable if volume growth moderates
Stock at fresh 52w high - much of beat already priced in
Heavy concentration in MAGGI/KITKAT/NESCAFE power brands
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 5/share record date July 10, 2026
Sector-wide bid following Nestle's blockbuster print
Q1 FY27 rural-demand and pricing print

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results NOT yet released - board meeting scheduled April 30, 2026 (3 days away) to approve Q4/FY26 results and consider final dividend. Analyst/investor presentation and earnings call scheduled same day. Analyst estimates: revenue Rs 15,200-15,600 cr, PAT Rs 2,500-2,700 cr. Q3 FY26 reported PAT Rs 6,603 cr (+121% YoY) inflated by Rs 4,611 cr one-time gain from ice cream business demerger; underlying PAT Rs 2,521 cr on revenue Rs 15,408 cr. FY25 final dividend was Rs 24/share (total FY25 ~Rs 53/share).
stocks.sector_context: FMCG sector outlook positive: CRISIL projects 6-8% revenue growth in FY26 vs 5-6% in FY25; supported by easing inflation, GST reforms, monetary easing (repo 5.25%), tax relief, benign commodities. Rural demand resilient and outpacing urban; urban recovery in progress. Nifty FMCG +2.55% on Apr 21 driven by Nestle Q4 beat - positive sentiment carry into HUL print.
stocks.peer_comparison: Largest FMCG by market cap (Rs 5.41 trn). Lower P/E (54.4x) vs Nestle 73.8x but higher than long-run average. Q4 estimates imply mid-single-digit growth - materially below Nestle's 22.6% revenue and ~18% volume blowout. Risk that HUL print disappoints on relative basis after Nestle set high bar.
red_flags: Imminent earnings event April 30 - 3-day risk window with high bar after Nestle's blockbuster
Underlying volume growth likely to lag Nestle materially - relative-performance disappointment risk
Post-demerger comparable base resets reported numbers - reading the print will be noisier
Analyst PAT estimate range (Rs 2,500-2,700 cr) only marginally above Q3 underlying - low headline growth
catalysts: April 30, 2026 board meeting: Q4/FY26 results AND final dividend
Earnings call same day - FY27 guidance and rural-demand commentary
Sector tailwinds: GST, rate cuts, rural recovery
